NCT01960348.name: APOLLO
NCT01960348.group: hATTR polyneuropathy, patisiran 0.3 mg/kg IV Q3W vs placebo (18 mo, mNIS+7 MD)

allOutcomes.title: mNIS+7 change from baseline at month 18 (primary, patisiran minus placebo, MD)

NCT01960348.snippet: Source: Adams D et al. NEJM 2018;379:11-21 (APOLLO).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 225 adults with hereditary transthyretin-mediated amyloidosis and polyneuropathy (NIS 5-130, mixed TTR genotypes, stages 1 and 2) randomized 2:1 double-blind to patisiran 0.3 mg/kg intravenous every 3 weeks (n=148) or matched placebo (n=77) for 18 months.

evidence.label: Primary - mNIS+7 at Month 18
evidence.text: Modified Neuropathy Impairment Score +7 (mNIS+7, range 0-304, higher = worse) change from baseline at month 18: patisiran -6.0 vs placebo +28.0; least-squares mean treatment difference -34.0 points (95% CI -39.9 to -28.1; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met with clinically meaningful halt/reversal of neuropathy progression.

evidence.label: Secondary - Norfolk QoL-DN and Cardiac Substudy
evidence.text: Norfolk QoL-DN treatment difference -21.1 (P<0.001). 10-metre walk test, mBMI, and COMPASS-31 all favour patisiran. Cardiac substudy (n=126): reduction in mean LV wall thickness (-0.9 mm vs +0.9 mm), NT-proBNP reduction, and reduced strain abnormality. TTR knockdown sustained >80%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by NIS, V30M genotype, and prior tetrabenazine use. D2 LOW - double-blind with matched saline placebo. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - central neurology exam training, blinded adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked prior to unblinding.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female of 18 to 85 years of age (inclusive);~* Have a diagnosis of FAP~* Neuropathy Impairment Score requirement of 5-130~* Meet Karnofsky performance status requirements~* Have adequate complete blood counts and liver function tests~* Have adequate cardiac function~* Have negative serology for hepatitis B virus (HBV) and hepatitis C virus (HCV)~Exclusion Criteria:~* Had a prior liver transplant or is planned to undergo liver transplant during the study period;~* H...

NCT03759379.name: HELIOS-A
NCT03759379.group: hATTR polyneuropathy, vutrisiran 25 mg SC Q3M vs external APOLLO placebo (18 mo, mNIS+7 MD)

allOutcomes.title: mNIS+7 change from baseline at month 18 (primary, vutrisiran minus external placebo, MD)

NCT03759379.snippet: Source: Adams D et al. Amyloid 2023;30:1-9 (HELIOS-A 18-mo).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 164 adults with hATTR polyneuropathy randomized 3:1 open-label to vutrisiran 25 mg subcutaneously every 3 months (n=122) or patisiran 0.3 mg/kg IV Q3W (n=42, reference arm). External placebo comparator used the APOLLO placebo arm (n=77) by prospective design for the primary comparison.

evidence.label: Primary - mNIS+7 at Month 18 (vs external APOLLO placebo)
evidence.text: mNIS+7 change from baseline at month 18: vutrisiran -0.46 vs external APOLLO placebo +28.09; least-squares mean treatment difference -28.55 points (95% CI -34.66 to -22.43; P<0.0001). Primary endpoint met. Non-inferiority to patisiran on serum TTR reduction demonstrated.

evidence.label: Secondary - Norfolk QoL-DN and Serum TTR
evidence.text: Norfolk QoL-DN LS mean diff -21.0 (P<0.0001). Timed 10-metre walk favoured vutrisiran. Serum TTR reduction >80% from baseline sustained through 18 months. Safety: injection-site reactions 5%, no liver function abnormalities, no renal signals.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label allocation (active comparator arm open-label; primary vs external APOLLO placebo raises risk of performance bias, partly mitigated by centrally adjudicated mNIS+7). D3 LOW - 97% completion. D4 LOW - central neurology exam training. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female of 18 to 85 years of age (inclusive);~* Has a diagnosis of hATTR amyloidosis with transthyretin (TTR) mutation;~* Has adequate neurologic impairment score (NIS);~* Has adequate polyneuropathy disability (PND) score;~* Has adequate Karnofsky Performance Status (KPS).~Exclusion Criteria:~* Had a prior liver transplant or is likely to undergo liver transplantation during the study;~* Has known other (non-hATTR) forms of amyloidosis or leptomeningeal amyloidosis...

title: RapidMeta Neurology | Patisiran and Vutrisiran (siRNA) for hATTR Polyneuropathy v1.1
